mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects broad economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns as output increases to meet demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a component of GDP
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a short period signals a sharp economic contraction and deteriorating consumer health, leading to lower earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve', increasing the probability of future aggressive tightening shocks that negatively impact equity valuations.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if equities rally consistently (>60% of triggered periods) despite the signal, suggesting markets anticipate a soft landing or disinflation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns, as borrowing costs are not a headwind.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle (e.g., FEDFUNDS falling over 6mo)
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS momentum and S&P 500 forward returns is zero or negative during periods where the FEDFUNDS rate has declined over the prior 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls growth is a primary indicator of economic health, boosting investor sentiment and expectations for consumer spending, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and tighter financial conditions, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacting equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector
failure_condition: The S&P 500 shows a positive median 60-day return in the periods following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate for future corporate earnings and increases borrowing costs, putting downward pressure on equity valuations over the very short term.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in FEDFUNDS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously slowing industrial production and accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary environment where corporate profit margins are squeezed from both lower demand and higher input costs, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of the periods where the trigger condition is met.
